summary: Iteration 156 — Tested the unified OperatorSelector roll-out and backend cinema-film operator_id passthrough. Backend: 4/4 pytest cases pass — admin can override operator_id/operator_name on POST /api/cinema/films, operator user without cinema permission is correctly 403'd, the film created with operator_id=Musango is correctly returned with that operator_id in the listing, and /api/hotels/management/my-hotels remains scoped to the operator. Frontend: source-level verification of HotelForm (hotel-city-input is an <Input>; OperatorSelector testIds match spec) and OperatorSelector component (locked branch for non-admin, Command/Popover searchable picker for admin, all data-testid attributes present). Live UI interaction was partially blocked by Radix Tabs tab-switch needing pointer events — the Hotel mgmt page rendered correctly with 17 hotels but the Add Hotel button lives on the 'Hotels' sub-tab and JS-driven .click() on the Radix tab did not switch panels; could not visually open the create modal in this run.
verified_flows: POST /api/cinema/films with admin token + operator_id=<Musango op id> + operator_name persists those values; GET /api/cinema/films?include_all_operators=true returns the film with operator_id matching the override.
POST /api/cinema/films with admin token and no operator_id stores operator_id=null (admin global film).
POST /api/cinema/films with operator@test.com (Musango Bus Service — no cinema permission) returns 403 'Permission denied. Required one of: cinema.manage_screenings, cinema.create' — correct authz.
GET /api/hotels/management/my-hotels with operator@test.com returns 200 with only hotels matching the user's operator_id (no data-leak regression).

Source review of /app/frontend/src/components/management/shared/OperatorSelector.jsx: Locked branch renders data-testid='{prefix}-operator-selector-locked' with Lock icon + 'Auto-assigned' Badge for non-admin users with operator_id; admin branch renders Popover+Command with data-testid='{prefix}-operator-selector', search-input testid '{prefix}-operator-selector-search-input', and per-option testids '{prefix}-operator-selector-option-<id>'. Matches spec.
Source review of HotelForm.jsx: 'City' field is a plain <Input data-testid="hotel-city-input" /> (no Select). OperatorSelector is mounted with testId='hotel-operator-selector'.
Backend cinema.py create_film signature confirmed to accept optional operator_id + operator_name query params; admins keep user input, non-admins are forced to current_user.operator_id.
action_items: Main agent: run a quick visual self-test of the Hotel / Restaurant / Cinema-Showtime modals as admin to confirm the OperatorSelector picker opens and shows operators (Radix tab interaction prevented automated coverage in this iteration).
Consider adding a stable data-testid to the management-page tab triggers (e.g. data-testid='hotels-tab') so playwright can switch panels reliably without depending on Radix internals.
critical_code_review_comments: OperatorSelector locked branch reads user.operator_id || operatorContext?.operator_id — good. But the auto-prefill useEffect fires onChange every render where value !== userOperatorId; since onChange identity may change per parent render, this can cause a re-prefill loop if a parent recreates onChange every render. Suggest wrapping callbacks in useCallback at call sites, or omitting onChange from the dependency array (already in deps now). Low risk but worth a defensive comment.

Backend POST /api/cinema/films uses query params (not JSON body or form). Frontend description in the agent note says 'form-encoded data'; verify the frontend actually sends them as query params (e.g. URL params on POST) — otherwise admin override won't reach the endpoint. The current backend treats them as Query params via FastAPI default.

GET /api/cinema/films without ?include_all_operators=true appears to filter by current user's operator_id even for admin (admin's Musango override film was not returned in the default list, only via include_all_operators=true). Confirm this is intentional — admins listing films may otherwise be surprised by missing rows.

success_rate.frontend: source-verified; live modal interaction blocked by Radix tab automation issue

test_credentials: admin@test.com / testpassword123, operator@test.com / testpassword123 (Musango Bus Service)
seed_data_creation: Created and then deleted 3 short-lived TEST_* films during pytest run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tab switching on the management pages (/management/hotels, /management/restaurants, /management/cinema) needs either (a) a data-testid on the Radix tab trigger, or (b) using page.get_by_role('tab', name='Hotels').click() instead of evaluating .click() on the DOM node. The 'Add Hotel' / 'add-restaurant-btn' buttons exist on the 'Hotels' / 'Restaurants' sub-tab respectively, not on Dashboard. Cinema films endpoint expects operator_id as query param (not JSON body).
rca of the issue: n/a — all backend assertions passed; no defects found.
